title: Reading is a System
subtitle: Read widely, deeply, and more effectively by treating reading as a system. Try building a cart with strategies that appeal to your goals.

strategies.title: Parallel Reading
strategies.subtitle: Read several sources at once to expose shared claims, tensions, and gaps.
strategies.body: Reading multiple books at once allows you to unlock many benifits that you would not get from reading one book at a time. You'll learn to discursively read, to make reading sessions more consistent, and to drop books caught in sunk cost fallacies.

strategies.title: You Won't Remember Everything You Read
strategies.subtitle: Make a Lookup Table to store and search for information you want to remember.
strategies.body: Our brains are not designed to remember everything we read. Instead, we should focus on remembering the information that is most relevant to our goals. A Lookup Table is a simple tool that allows you to store and search for information you want to remember.
